vibe_description: The Cathedral of Football. Azteca's roar echoes through football history. Deafening home support, a wall of noise, street food on every corner, and a city that bleeds football.
transport_tips: Metro Line 2 to Estadio Azteca. Uber recommended from Roma/Condesa.

vibe_description: Monterrey is industrial Mexico with a fierce football soul. BBVA sits in the shadow of the Sierra Madre. Craft beer, carne asada, and a crowd that never stops singing.
transport_tips: Metro to Exposicion, then shuttle. Taxis from Barrio Antiguo.

vibe_description: Guadalajara is the birthplace of tequila and mariachi. Akron feels like a party before kickoff. Colourful, festive, and the best street tacos in Mexico.
transport_tips: Light Rail from city center. Uber widely available.

vibe_description: The World Cup Final venue. New York's energy amplified to eleven. A melting pot of fans from every nation, Times Square fan fests, and the skyline as your backdrop.
transport_tips: NJ Transit train from Penn Station to Meadowlands. Buses from Port Authority.

vibe_description: LA is sunshine, diversity, and spectacle. SoFi is stunning. Expect celebrity sightings, Hollywood fan fests, and the most photogenic stadium in the tournament.
transport_tips: Metro C Line to Inglewood. Rideshare from WeHo/Downtown.

fan_zones: Dallas Fan Fest at Fair Park
vibe_description: Everything is bigger in Texas. AT&T Stadium is an arena of biblical scale with the world's largest retractable HD screen. BBQ pits, country crossover, and football in its purest Texan form.
transport_tips: Trinity Railway Express to CentrePort, then shuttle. Uber from Dallas.

vibe_description: Silicon Valley meets the beautiful game. The Bay Area's cosmopolitan mix makes for a uniquely intelligent, passionate crowd. Fog-free game days in the South Bay.
transport_tips: VTA Light Rail to Great America Station. Caltrain to Santa Clara.

vibe_description: Arrowhead is one of the loudest stadiums on Earth. Kansas City tailgate culture is legendary — arrive 4 hours early. World's best BBQ is right outside the gates.
transport_tips: Bus from Downtown or drive and tailgate. Limited transit options.

vibe_description: Houston is the most diverse city in America and it will show. NRG pulses with Latin energy, plus air conditioning in the retractable roof — crucial in Texas summer.
transport_tips: METRORail Red Line to Reliant Park. Park & Ride from downtown.

vibe_description: Philly fans are the most passionate and the most intense in America. The city of brotherly love has a rocky sporting soul. Expect a wall of noise and cheesesteaks on every corner.
transport_tips: SEPTA Broad Street Line to Pattison.

vibe_description: Boston feels like it was born for the World Cup. European pub culture, historic cobblestone streets, and a city that truly understands what this tournament means.
transport_tips: Commuter Rail from South Station to Foxboro. Shuttle buses from Boston.

vibe_description: Seattle is effortlessly cool. Lumen Field overlooks Elliott Bay with mountain backdrops. The city's strong immigrant community creates a unique multicultural football vibe.
transport_tips: King County Metro or Link Light Rail to Stadium Station.

vibe_description: Miami brings South American football culture to US soil. Hard Rock bakes in tropical heat with 70% of the crowd speaking Spanish. Post-game parties on South Beach are mandatory.
transport_tips: Miami Metrorail to Palmetto, then shuttle. Uber from South Beach.

vibe_description: Las Vegas stakes everything on spectacle and this World Cup will be no different. The Strip doesn't sleep, the neon never dims, and your World Cup memories will last a lifetime.
transport_tips: Las Vegas Monorail plus rideshare. Walking from Strip hotels possible.

vibe_description: Toronto is the most multicultural city on Earth and the stadium will reflect it. Every language, every flag, every nation sharing the beautiful game by Lake Ontario.
transport_tips: Streetcar 509 to Exhibition Loop from Union Station.

vibe_description: BC Place sits with the North Shore Mountains as a backdrop. Vancouver's Pacific Rim culture means Asian football passion with South Korean, Japanese, and Australian fan bases all strong.
transport_tips: SkyTrain to Stadium-Chinatown. Walk from Downtown.
